Tests the unary negation operator with a custom `valueOf` method in an object.

# Purpose
This code defines an object `a` with a method `valueOf` that returns `-1`. The `valueOf` method is a built-in JavaScript method that returns the primitive value of an object. In this context, it is overridden to return `-1` when the object `a` is used in a numeric context. The code then uses an `assert` statement to verify that the negation of `a` results in `1`, demonstrating the custom behavior of the `valueOf` method. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on demonstrating and testing the behavior of the `valueOf` method in JavaScript.
# Global Variables

---
### a
- **Type**: ``object``
- **Description**: Contains a single method `valueOf` that returns the integer `-1`. This method is used to define the behavior of the object when it is converted to a primitive value.
- **Use**: Used to override the default behavior of the unary negation operator, resulting in the expression `-a` evaluating to `1`.
